titleStmt.title: Bǝḥerāwi Kǝllǝlāwi Mangǝśti Tǝgrāy, Dabra Dāmmo ʾAbuna ʾAragāwi, DD-001
Taʾammǝra Māryām "Miracles of Mary"

publicationStmt.publisher: Die Schriftkultur des christlichen Äthiopiens und Eritreas: Eine multimediale Forschungsumgebung / Beta maṣāḥǝft

licence.p: This file is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0.

msContents.summary: I) Taʾammǝra Māryām "Miracles of Mary" (fols. 3ra-121va) I-1a) Taʾammǝra Māryām "Miracles of Mary": 23 miracles (fols. 3ra-15va, 105va-121va) I-2) ʾƎsaggǝd laki wa-ʾǝweddǝsaki... "I worship Thee..." (fols. 16ra-vb) I-1b) Taʾammǝra Māryām "Miracles of Mary": 34 miracles (fols. 17ra-105va) I-3) ʾAkkonu bǝʾsi... “Does not the man...” (distributed among the miracles of Mary, fols. 18vb-92rb).

msItem.title: ʾAkkonu bǝʾsi... “Does not the man...”
msItem.note: Distributed among the miracles of Mary, fols. 18vb-92rb.

supportDesc.extent: Made of 123.0 folios, of which blank 1r, 5v, 6v, 7v, 8v, 9v, 15vb, 19rb, 23rb, 31rb, 40v, 47v, 50r, 58v, 82v, 88v,90v, 92r, 94v, 121vb-123vb., in 16.0 quires . 320 296 70
supportDesc.condition: The Ms. has probably been resewn.

layout.ab: The upper line is written above the ruling.
The bottom line is written above the ruling.

handNote.seg: Hand a: mediocre. The script is characterized by right-sloping, angular letters with short hairlines.
Black, red (hand a and c: strong red, hand b: vivid red).
handNote.date: Hand a: late 19th century (?). Hand b: 19th century (?). Hand c: mid 17th century.
handNote.seg: Divine names; names of the donors of Texts I-1a, incipit of sections; elements of the punctuation signs and of the text dividers; elements of Ethiopic numerals.
Hand b (1-3 and 1-4) careful. The script is characterized by small, rounded and upright letters.
Hand c: neat and well trained. The handwriting appears very regular throughout the Ms. with finely shaped, tall, slender and upright letters.

decoDesc.decoNote:
Fol. 2r: Mary's prayer at Golgotha. Apparition of Our Lord to Mary.Legends: ዘከመ፡ ጸለየት፡ እግዝእትነ፡ ማርያም፡ በጎልጎታ። ዘከመ፡ ሐወጻ፡ እግዝእነ፡ ለእግዝእትነ፡ ማርያም፡ እንዘ፡ ትጼሊ፡ በጎልጎታ።
Many miniatures are protected by a textile curtain.
Fol. 2v: Ascension of Mary.Legends: ዘከመ፡ አዕረግዋ፡ መላእክት፡ ለእግዝእትነ፡ ማርያም፡ እምጎልጎታ፡ ውስተ፡ ሰማይ። ዘከመ፡ ሰገዱ፡ ላቲ፡ ለእግዝእትነ፡ ነፍሳት፡ ጻድቃን፡ ምስለ፡ አብርሃም፡ ይስሐቅል፡ ወያዕቆብ።
Fol 4r: Covenant of Mercy (in heaven).Legends: ዘከመ፡ አዕረጋ፡ እግዚእነ፡ ለእግዚእትነ፡ ማርያም፡ ወአኃዛ፡ በእዴሁ፡ ወአንበራ፡ ወወሀባ፡ ኪዳነ። እንዘ፡ ይብል፡ ዳዊት፡ አቡሃ፡ ስምዒ፡ ወልትየ፡ ወርእዪ፡ ወአጽምዒ፡ እዝነኪ፡ ርስዒ፡ ሕዝበኪ፡ ወቤተ፡ አቡኪ።
Fol. 5r: Angels showing the fire of Sheol to Mary.Legend: ዘከመ፡ አርእይዋ፡ መላእክት፡ ለእግዝእትነ፡ ማርያም፡ እሳተ፡ ሲኦል፡ ወዘውስቴቱ፡ ነፍሳተ፡ ኃጥአን።
Fol. 6r: The Virgin grieving about sinners.Legends: በእንተ፡ ዘከመ፡ ኮነ፡ ትቴክዝ፡ ወታስተሐምም፡ እግዝእትነ፡ ማርያም፡ በእንተ፡ ኃጥአን። ዘከመ፡ ጸለየት፡ እግዝእትነ፡ ማርያም፡ በዊአ፡ ውስተ፡ ቅጽረ፡ ቀራንዮ፡ እምድኅረ፡ አርአይዋ፡ ለሲኦል፡ ወለጽልመት።
Fol. 7r: Covenant of Mercy (on earth).Legend: ዘከመ፡ ወረደ፡ እግዚእነ፡ እምሰማይ፡ ምስለ፡ አእላፍ፡ መላእክት፡ እንዘ፡ ትጼሊ፡ በቀራንዮ፡ እግዝእትነ፡ ማርያም፡ አመ፡ ፲ወ፯ ለየካቲት፡ ወወሀባ፡ ኪዳነ፡ ምሕረት።
Fol. 8r: Annunciation.Legend: ዘከመ፡ አብሰራ፡ ገብርኤል፡ መልአክ፡ ለእግዝእትነ፡ ማርያም።
Fol. 10r: Flight into EgyptLegend: ዘከመ፡ ጐየት፡ እግዝእትነ፡ ማርያም፡ ውስተ፡ ደብረ፡ ቊስቋም፡ እመዓተ፡ ሄሮድስ፡ ምስለ፡ ዮሴፍ፡ ወሰሎሜ።

Fol. 19v: Bishop Dexius (Däqsǝyos) writing the book of Miracles of Mary and receiving a throne and a garment from Maryዘከመ፡ ጸሐፈ፡ ደቅስዮስ፡ መጽሐፈ፡ ተአምሪሃ፡ ለእግዝእተ፡ ኵልነ፡ ወመጠዋ። ዘከመ፡ ተመጠወቶ፡ መጽሐፈ፡ ተአምሪሃ፡ እግዝእትነ፡ እምእደ፡ ደቅስዮስ፡ ወመጠወቶ፡ ዳግመ፡ ወባረከቶ። ዘከመ፡ ወሀበቶ፡ መንበር፡ ወልብስ፡ ለደቅስዮስ፡ እግዝእትነ። Cp. One Hundred and Ten Miracles of Our Lady Mary, Translated from Ethiopic Manuscripts for the Most Part in the British Museum, with Extracts from Some Ancient European Versions, and Illustrations from the Paintings in Manuscripts by Ethiopian Artists, with Sixty-Four PlatesErnest Alfred WallisBudgeLondonOxford University Press–Humphrey Milford1933https://archive.org/details/onehundredtenmir0000budg/mode/2up?view=theaterplates I, II

Fol. 20r: Dexius and Mary. An Angel punishing a bishop who seated himself on the throne of Dexius.Legends: ዘከመ፡ ነበረ፡ ደቅስዮስ፡ ዲበ፡ መንበር፡ ዘወሀበቶ፡ እግዝእትነ። ዘከመ፡ ያነብብ፡ ደቅስዮስ፡ መጽሐፈ፡ ተአምሪሃ፡ ለእግዝእትነ። ዘከመ፡ ተዓርቀት፡ መንበር፡ ወልብስ፡ ሶበ፡ ወደቀ፡ ኤጲስ፡ ቆጶስ። ዘከመ፡ አወደቆ፡ መልአክ፡ እመንበረ፡ ደቅስዮስ፡ ኤጲስቆጶስ፡ ወቀሠፎ። One Hundred and Ten Miracles of Our Lady Mary, Translated from Ethiopic Manuscripts for the Most Part in the British Museum, with Extracts from Some Ancient European Versions, and Illustrations from the Paintings in Manuscripts by Ethiopian Artists, with Sixty-Four PlatesErnest Alfred WallisBudgeLondonOxford University Press–Humphrey Milford1933https://archive.org/details/onehundredtenmir0000budg/mode/2up?view=theaterplate II
Fol. 21v: An artisan greeting Mary.ዘከመ፡ ወሀባ፡ ገባራዊ፡ ሰላመ፡ መልአክ፡ ለእግዝእትነ፡ ወሶበ፡ በጽሐ፡ ኀበ፡ ይብል፡ ቡርከት፡ አንቲ፡ እምአንስት። ዘከመ፡ ተሠጥወቶ፡ እግዝእትነ፡ ወትቤሎ፡ ቡሩክ፡ አንተኒ፡ እምእደው፡ እንዘ፡ ታኤምሮ፡ በአጻብዕታ።

Fol. 23v: A Jew bowing down before Mary.Legends: ፪ ዲያቆናት፡ ዘደመሮሙ፡ ኤጲስ፡ ቆጶስ፡ ከመ፡ ይትራድዕዎ። ዘከመ፡ ወድቀ፡ ዕብራዊ፡ እንዘ፡ ይሰግድ፡ ለእግዝእትነ። ዘከመ፡ አንሥአቶ፡ እግዝእትነ፡ ለዕብራዊ፡ ሶበ፡ ወድቀ፡ እንዘ፡ ይሰግድ፡ ላቲ። ዘከመ፡ ቆመ፡ ዕብራዊ። ዘከመ፡ አመረቶ፡ እግዝእትነ፡ ለዕብራዊ፡ ከመ፡ ይቁም፡ በየማነ፡ ምሥዋዕ፡ ወበየማና። Cp. One Hundred and Ten Miracles of Our Lady Mary, Translated from Ethiopic Manuscripts for the Most Part in the British Museum, with Extracts from Some Ancient European Versions, and Illustrations from the Paintings in Manuscripts by Ethiopian Artists, with Sixty-Four PlatesErnest Alfred WallisBudgeLondonOxford University Press–Humphrey Milford1933https://archive.org/details/onehundredtenmir0000budg/mode/2up?view=theaterplates III, IV, V

Fol. 25v: Mary and Damianus (Dǝmǝyānos)Legends: ዘከመ፡ ጸሐፈ፡ ስማ፡ ድምኖስ[sic]፡ ለእግዝእትነ። ዘከመ፡ አብሰረቶ፡ እግዝእትነ፡ ለድምያኖስ። አምደ፡ ወርቅ። Cp. One Hundred and Ten Miracles of Our Lady Mary, Translated from Ethiopic Manuscripts for the Most Part in the British Museum, with Extracts from Some Ancient European Versions, and Illustrations from the Paintings in Manuscripts by Ethiopian Artists, with Sixty-Four PlatesErnest Alfred WallisBudgeLondonOxford University Press–Humphrey Milford1933https://archive.org/details/onehundredtenmir0000budg/mode/2up?view=theaterplates VI, VII

Fol. 27v: Mary and Abbas, bishop of Rome.Legends: ዘከመ፡ መተረ፡ እዴሁ፡ አባስ። ዘከመ፡ አስተጣግዓት፡ እዴሁ፡ ዘተመትረ፡ ለአባስ፡ ወፈወሰቶ፡ እግዝእትነ፡ ማርያም። Cp. One Hundred and Ten Miracles of Our Lady Mary, Translated from Ethiopic Manuscripts for the Most Part in the British Museum, with Extracts from Some Ancient European Versions, and Illustrations from the Paintings in Manuscripts by Ethiopian Artists, with Sixty-Four PlatesErnest Alfred WallisBudgeLondonOxford University Press–Humphrey Milford1933https://archive.org/details/onehundredtenmir0000budg/mode/2up?view=theaterplates VIII, IX, X

Fol. 29v: Mary and Isaac.Legends: ዘከመ፡ ወድቀ፡ ይስሐቅ፡ ሶበ፡ ርእየ፡ ገጸ፡ እግዝእትነ። ዘከመ፡ አንሥአቶ፡ እግዝእትነ። Cp. One Hundred and Ten Miracles of Our Lady Mary, Translated from Ethiopic Manuscripts for the Most Part in the British Museum, with Extracts from Some Ancient European Versions, and Illustrations from the Paintings in Manuscripts by Ethiopian Artists, with Sixty-Four PlatesErnest Alfred WallisBudgeLondonOxford University Press–Humphrey Milford1933https://archive.org/details/onehundredtenmir0000budg/mode/2up?view=theaterplate XI

Fol. 31v: Mary and the child Mary.Legend: ይእቲ፡ ወለት። ʾArke-hymn: መቅደሰ፡ ሥላሴ፡ ማርያም፡ ዘተገልበብኪ፡ ስብሐተ፡ እንዘ፡ በረከትኪ፡ ይጸወር፡ ወአሣዕነኪ፡ ድርገተ፡ ወልደ፡ ክርስቶስ፡ ገብርኪ፡ አቅመ፡ ተመነየ፡ ትምኔተ፡ ጥቡዓ፡ ከመ፡ ይትሉኪ፡ መንገለ፡ ሖርኪ፡ ፍኖተ፡ ብሂላተ፡ እንዘ፡ አስምዒዮ፡ ወሥመሪ፡ ዘንተ። Cp. One Hundred and Ten Miracles of Our Lady Mary, Translated from Ethiopic Manuscripts for the Most Part in the British Museum, with Extracts from Some Ancient European Versions, and Illustrations from the Paintings in Manuscripts by Ethiopian Artists, with Sixty-Four PlatesErnest Alfred WallisBudgeLondonOxford University Press–Humphrey Milford1933https://archive.org/details/onehundredtenmir0000budg/mode/2up?view=theaterplate XII

Fol. 33v: Mary and the painter whose scaffold was overthrown by the devil.Legends: ዘከመ፡ ሠዓለ፡ ሠዓሊ፡ ገነተ፡ ማኅደረ፡ ጸድቃን፡ ወእሳተ፡ ሲኦል፡ ማኅደረ፡ ዲያብሎስ፡ ወሠራዊቱ። ዘከመ፡ ተነጽሐ፡ ብእሲ፡ ሠዓሊ፡ እምዓራት። ዘከመ፡ አንሥአቶ፡ እግዝእትነ። Cp. One Hundred and Ten Miracles of Our Lady Mary, Translated from Ethiopic Manuscripts for the Most Part in the British Museum, with Extracts from Some Ancient European Versions, and Illustrations from the Paintings in Manuscripts by Ethiopian Artists, with Sixty-Four PlatesErnest Alfred WallisBudgeLondonOxford University Press–Humphrey Milford1933https://archive.org/details/onehundredtenmir0000budg/mode/2up?view=theaterplate XIII

Fol. 35r: Mary and the sick pilgrim.Legend: ዘከመ፡ አብጽሐቶ፡ እግዝእትነ፡ ለነጋዲ፡ ኀበ፡ ኢየሩሳሌም። Cp. One Hundred and Ten Miracles of Our Lady Mary, Translated from Ethiopic Manuscripts for the Most Part in the British Museum, with Extracts from Some Ancient European Versions, and Illustrations from the Paintings in Manuscripts by Ethiopian Artists, with Sixty-Four PlatesErnest Alfred WallisBudgeLondonOxford University Press–Humphrey Milford1933https://archive.org/details/onehundredtenmir0000budg/mode/2up?view=theaterplate XIV

Fol. 35v: Mary baptizing the sick pilgrim in the Jordan.Legend: ዘከመ፡ አጥመቆቶ፡ በዮርዳኖስ፡ ለነጋዲ፡ ሕሙም፡ እግዝእትነ፡ ማርያም። Cp. One Hundred and Ten Miracles of Our Lady Mary, Translated from Ethiopic Manuscripts for the Most Part in the British Museum, with Extracts from Some Ancient European Versions, and Illustrations from the Paintings in Manuscripts by Ethiopian Artists, with Sixty-Four PlatesErnest Alfred WallisBudgeLondonOxford University Press–Humphrey Milford1933https://archive.org/details/onehundredtenmir0000budg/mode/2up?view=theaterplate XV

Fol. 38r: Mary and the youth Zacharias (Zakārǝyās).Legends: ዘከመ፡ አስተቀጸላ፡ ጽጌ፡ ረዳ፡ ለእግዝእትነ፡ ማርያም፡ ዘካርያስ፡ ወሬዛ። ዘከመ፡ ሰገደ፡ ዘካርያስ፡ ቅድመ፡ ሥዕላ።
ዘከመ፡ ጸለየ፡ ዘካርያስ፡ በሰላመ፡ መልአክ።

Fol. 38v: Mary, Zacharias (Zakārǝyās) and the thieves.Legends: ዘከመ፡ ትትሜጠው፡ እግዝእትነ፡ ጽጌ፡ ረዳ፡ እምአፈ፡ ዘካርያስ። ፈያታወያን። Cp. One Hundred and Ten Miracles of Our Lady Mary, Translated from Ethiopic Manuscripts for the Most Part in the British Museum, with Extracts from Some Ancient European Versions, and Illustrations from the Paintings in Manuscripts by Ethiopian Artists, with Sixty-Four PlatesErnest Alfred WallisBudgeLondonOxford University Press–Humphrey Milford1933https://archive.org/details/onehundredtenmir0000budg/mode/2up?view=theaterplates XVI, XVII

Fol. 40r: Mary with Barbara and Juliana (Yolyānos).Legends: እሉኒ፡ ፈያት፡ ጥረሲሆሙ፡ ዘተሰብረ። ባርባራ፡ ወዮልያኖስ። እሉ፡ ፈያት፡ ዘመንኰሱ። Cp. One Hundred and Ten Miracles of Our Lady Mary, Translated from Ethiopic Manuscripts for the Most Part in the British Museum, with Extracts from Some Ancient European Versions, and Illustrations from the Paintings in Manuscripts by Ethiopian Artists, with Sixty-Four PlatesErnest Alfred WallisBudgeLondonOxford University Press–Humphrey Milford1933https://archive.org/details/onehundredtenmir0000budg/mode/2up?view=theaterplates XVIII and XIX
Fol. 42v: Mary and the three Arabs.Legends: ፫ዓረባውያን፡ ዘፈቀደ፡ ያሥጥሞሙ፡ ባሕር። ዘከመ፡ ረከቡ፡ ኰኵሐ፡ ወድኅኑ። ፩ዓረባዊ፡ ዘአምጽአ፡ ፩ገመል፡ ለመፍቅደ፡ ደብር። ካልኡ፡ ዓረባዊ፡ ዘወሀበ፡ ጸጕረ፡ በግዕ፡ ለደብር። ወገመልሂ፡ ዘወሀበ፡ ለደብር። Cp.

Fol. 44r: Mary appearing to a priest in a dream.Legends: ደብረ፡ ዕቆና፡ ዘተሐንጸት፡ በሀገረ፡ ጽርዕ፡ በከመ፡ እግዝእትነ፡ ማርያም። ዘከመ፡ ወጽኡ፡ ሰብእ፡ ወዘከመ፡ ደንገጹ፡ ሶበ፡ ነጽርዋ፡ ለደብረ፡ ዕቆና፡ በጽንፈ፡ ባሕረ፡ ኢያረኮ። ዘከመ፡ ነገረቶ፡ ለ፩ ቀሲስ፡ በሕልም። Cp. One Hundred and Ten Miracles of Our Lady Mary, Translated from Ethiopic Manuscripts for the Most Part in the British Museum, with Extracts from Some Ancient European Versions, and Illustrations from the Paintings in Manuscripts by Ethiopian Artists, with Sixty-Four PlatesErnest Alfred WallisBudgeLondonOxford University Press–Humphrey Milford1933https://archive.org/details/onehundredtenmir0000budg/mode/2up?view=theaterplate XXII

Fol. 44v: Mary transporting the monastery to the side of a stream.Legend: [...] ዘአፍለስታ፡ እግዝእትነ፡ ኀበ፡ ጽንፈ፡ ባሕረ፡ ኢያሪኮ፡ በአሐቲ፡ ሰዓተ፡ ሌሊት፡ አመ፡ ፳ወ፫፡ ዘነሓሴ፡ ወእሉኒ። ሰብእ፡ ንዋማን፡ ዘነቅሑ፡ በሳኒታሁ፡ ወደንገፁ፡ ወገብረ፡ ዓውያተ። Cp. One Hundred and Ten Miracles of Our Lady Mary, Translated from Ethiopic Manuscripts for the Most Part in the British Museum, with Extracts from Some Ancient European Versions, and Illustrations from the Paintings in Manuscripts by Ethiopian Artists, with Sixty-Four PlatesErnest Alfred WallisBudgeLondonOxford University Press–Humphrey Milford1933https://archive.org/details/onehundredtenmir0000budg/mode/2up?view=theaterplate XXIII
Fol. 47r: Mary and priest John (Yoḥannǝs)Legends: ዘከመ፡ ጸለየ፡ ዮሐንስ፡ ቀሲስ፡ እንዘ፡ ሀሎ፡ ዕውረ፡ ቅድመ፡ ሥዕለ፡ እግዝእትነ፡ ማርያም። ዘከመ፡ አውፅአት፡ እጥባቲሃ፡ እግዝእትነ፡ ማርያም፡ ወአንጠብጠበት፡ ሐሊበ፡ ወቀብዓቶ፡ አዕይንቲሁ፡ በአዕባእታ፡ ወርእየ፡ ሶቤሃ፡ ዮሐንስ።

Fol. 49v: Mary healing the eye of a nobleman.Legends: ዘከመ፡ ሖረ፡ በሐመር፡ ባዕል፡ ጽርዓዊ፡ ብሔረ፡ ትርኵ፡ ከመ፡ ይጽብኦሙ። ሰብአ፡ ቍዝ፡ እለ፡ ፀብእዎ፡ ለባዕል። ዘከመ፡ ተነድፈ፡ በሐፅ፡ ባዕል፡ ጽርዓዊ። ዘከመ፡ ወድቀ፡ ሐፅ፡ እምዓይነ፡ ምስል፡ ወእምዓይነ፡ ባዕል፡ በአሐተ፡ ጊዜ፡ ወተፈወሰ። Cp. One Hundred and Ten Miracles of Our Lady Mary, Translated from Ethiopic Manuscripts for the Most Part in the British Museum, with Extracts from Some Ancient European Versions, and Illustrations from the Paintings in Manuscripts by Ethiopian Artists, with Sixty-Four PlatesErnest Alfred WallisBudgeLondonOxford University Press–Humphrey Milford1933https://archive.org/details/onehundredtenmir0000budg/mode/2up?view=theaterplates XXIV, XXV, XXVI

Fol. 52v: Mary anointing the eyes of a blind daughter with milk.Legends: ብእሲት፡ ዘስማ፡ ጌራ፡ አንስት፡ ዘከመ፡ ጸለየት፡ ቅድመ፡ ሥዕላ፡ ለእግዝእትነ፡ ማርያም፡ ምስለ፡ ወለታ፡ ዕወርት። ዘከመ፡ ኖሙ፡ እም፡ ወወለት። ዘከመ፡ አንቀሐታ፡ እግዝእትነ።
ዘከመ፡ ከሠተት፡ አዕይንቲሃ፡ በሐሊባ። Cp. One Hundred and Ten Miracles of Our Lady Mary, Translated from Ethiopic Manuscripts for the Most Part in the British Museum, with Extracts from Some Ancient European Versions, and Illustrations from the Paintings in Manuscripts by Ethiopian Artists, with Sixty-Four PlatesErnest Alfred WallisBudgeLondonOxford University Press–Humphrey Milford1933https://archive.org/details/onehundredtenmir0000budg/mode/2up?view=theaterplates XXVII, XXVIII, XXIX

Fol. 54v: Mary and the three poor daughters of ʿƎberayit.Legends: ዘከመ፡ ጸለየት፡ ዕቤራዪት፡ ኀበ፡ እግዝእትነ፡ በእንተ፡ ንዴቶን፡ ለሠላስ፡ አዋልዲሃ። እሎን፡ እማንቱ፡ አዋልዲሃ፡ ለዕቤራዪት፡ ቀዳሚት፡ ማርያም፡ ወዳግሚት፡ ማርታ፡ ወሣልሲት፡ የዋሂት። ማርያም፡ ሞተት፡ ወለክልኤቱ፡ ነሥኦን፡ ቴዎግ፡ ፀራቢ። ዘከመ፡ ገነዝዋ፡ ለወለተ፡ ዕቤራይተ፡ ዘከመ፡ ነገረታ፡ እግዝእትነ። ዘከመ፡ ነሥኦን፡ ዐራቢ፡ ወወሰዶን፡ ውስተ፡ ቤቱ፡ ለአዋልደ፡ ዕቤራዪት፡ ለማርታ፡ ወየዋሂት። Cp. One Hundred and Ten Miracles of Our Lady Mary, Translated from Ethiopic Manuscripts for the Most Part in the British Museum, with Extracts from Some Ancient European Versions, and Illustrations from the Paintings in Manuscripts by Ethiopian Artists, with Sixty-Four PlatesErnest Alfred WallisBudgeLondonOxford University Press–Humphrey Milford1933https://archive.org/details/onehundredtenmir0000budg/mode/2up?view=theaterplates XXX, XXXI

Fol. 56v: Mary rescuing the scribe's soul.Legends: ፩ጸሐፊ፡ እም፪ጸሐፍያን፡ ዘነሥኡ፡ ነፍሶ፡ መላእክተ፡ ጽልመት። ፪አኃው፡ ጸሐፍያን። ዘከመ፡ መጤቶ፡ እግዝእትነ፡ ለዝኩ፡ ጸሐፊ፡ ኀበ፡ መካኑ፡ ዘቀዳሚ፡ ወዘከመ፡ ወጠነ፡ ይጽሕፍ። ዘከመ፡ አወራደታ፡ እግዝእትነ፡ ለነፍሰ፡ ጸሐፊ፡ እኂዞ፡ በእዴሃ፡ እምእደ፡ አጋንንት።

Fol. 58r: Mary visiting George in prison.Legends: ዘከመ፡ ጸለለቶ፡ እግዝእትነ፡ ማርያም፡ ክነፊሃ፡ በእምሳለ፡ ርግብ፡ እንዘ፡ ሀሎ፡ በቤተ፡ ሞቅሕ። ዘከመ፡ ተፈወሰ፡ ጊዮርጊስ፡ ሐዲስ። Cp. One Hundred and Ten Miracles of Our Lady Mary, Translated from Ethiopic Manuscripts for the Most Part in the British Museum, with Extracts from Some Ancient European Versions, and Illustrations from the Paintings in Manuscripts by Ethiopian Artists, with Sixty-Four PlatesErnest Alfred WallisBudgeLondonOxford University Press–Humphrey Milford1933https://archive.org/details/onehundredtenmir0000budg/mode/2up?view=theaterplate XXXII

Fol. 60v: A church's guard punished and priest Käṭir healed by Mary.Legends: ዘከመ፡ ተበአሶ፡ ዓቃቢ፡ ለቀሲስ፡ ከጢር፡ እንዘ፡ ይደጕፆ፡ ዓይኖ፡ ወይቤሎ፡ ፃእ፡ እምደብር። ዘአዘዘቶ፡ መልአክ፡ ወአጽበሶ፡ እግሮ፡ ለዓቃቢ። ዓቃቢ፡ ዘኑነ[sic]፡ ፅቡስ፡ በመቅሠፍተ፡ መልአክ። ዘከመ፡ ተፈወሳ፡ ከጢር፡ ወቆመ፡ ርቱዓ፡ ውስተ፡ ቤተ፡ ክርስቲያን። Cp. One Hundred and Ten Miracles of Our Lady Mary, Translated from Ethiopic Manuscripts for the Most Part in the British Museum, with Extracts from Some Ancient European Versions, and Illustrations from the Paintings in Manuscripts by Ethiopian Artists, with Sixty-Four PlatesErnest Alfred WallisBudgeLondonOxford University Press–Humphrey Milford1933https://archive.org/details/onehundredtenmir0000budg/mode/2up?view=theaterplate XXXIII

Fol. 63r: A Muslim receiving a garment from Nazib and accusing him falsely before the Qadi.Legends: ዘከመ፡ ሰአሎሙ፡ ተንበለታዊ፡ ለ፪አኃው፡ የአልሉ፡ ሎቱ፡ ልብሰ። ዘከመ፡ ሰዓመ፡ ርእሶ፡ ተንበለታዊ፡ ለነዚብ፡ ሶበ፡ አለለ፡ ሎቱ፡ ልብሰ፡ ነዚብ። ዘከመ፡ ነገሮ፡ ወአስተዋደዮ፡ ተንበለታዊ፡ ለነዚብ፡ ኀበ፡ ቃዲ። ዘከመ፡ ወደዮ፡ ቃዲ፡ ለነዚብ፡ ውስተ፡ ቤተ፡ ሞቅሕ።
Cp. One Hundred and Ten Miracles of Our Lady Mary, Translated from Ethiopic Manuscripts for the Most Part in the British Museum, with Extracts from Some Ancient European Versions, and Illustrations from the Paintings in Manuscripts by Ethiopian Artists, with Sixty-Four PlatesErnest Alfred WallisBudgeLondonOxford University Press–Humphrey Milford1933https://archive.org/details/onehundredtenmir0000budg/mode/2up?view=theaterplates XXXIV, XXXV

Fol. 63v: St. George releasing Nazib from prison.Legends: ዘከመ፡ አፍርሆ፡ ቅዱስ፡ ጊዮርጊስ፡ ለቃዲ፡ ወአዘዘ፡ ከመ፡ ይፍትሖ፡ ለነዚብ፡ ወፈትሖ። ዘከመ፡ ጸሐፈ፡ ቃዲ፡ ጦማረ፡ ወወሀቦ፡ ለነዚብ፡ ትእምርተ፡ ወፈነዎ፡ ለነዚብ፡ ኀበ፡ ሀገሩ። ዘከመ፡ ቦአ፡ ነዚብ፡ ውስተ፡ ቤተ፡ ክርስቲያን፡ ወመጠዎሙ፡ ጦማረ፡ ዘወሀቦ፡ ቃዲ፡ ወአንበብዋ፡ ካህናት። Cp. One Hundred and Ten Miracles of Our Lady Mary, Translated from Ethiopic Manuscripts for the Most Part in the British Museum, with Extracts from Some Ancient European Versions, and Illustrations from the Paintings in Manuscripts by Ethiopian Artists, with Sixty-Four PlatesErnest Alfred WallisBudgeLondonOxford University Press–Humphrey Milford1933https://archive.org/details/onehundredtenmir0000budg/mode/2up?view=theaterplates XXXVI, XXXVII

Fol. 65v: The patriarch ordering the people to leave the church because of a man suffering from elephantitis.Legends: ዘከመ፡ ተመይጡ፡ ተመጣውያነ፡ ቍርባን፡ በትእዛዘ፡ ሊቀ፡ ጳጳሳት፡ ያርእዩ፡ እግሮሙ፡ እስመ፡ ተጐድዓት፡ ቤተ፡ ክርስቲያን። ዘከመ፡ ተፈልጠ፡ እምሕዝብ፡ ወጐየ፡ ዘእብን፡ እግሩ።

Fol. 66r: Mary healing the foot of the ill man.Legends: ዘከመ፡ ሰአለ፡ ኀቤሃ፡ ዘእብን፡ እግሩ። ዘከመ፡ ተፈወሰ፡ ብእሲ፡ ዘእብን፡ እግሩ። ዘከመ፡ ቀልአ፡ አልባሲሁ፡ ዘእብን፡ እግሩ፡ ወአርአዮ፡ ለሊቀ፡ ጳጳሳት፡ ዘከመ፡ ኮነ፡ ሕያወ። Cp. One Hundred and Ten Miracles of Our Lady Mary, Translated from Ethiopic Manuscripts for the Most Part in the British Museum, with Extracts from Some Ancient European Versions, and Illustrations from the Paintings in Manuscripts by Ethiopian Artists, with Sixty-Four PlatesErnest Alfred WallisBudgeLondonOxford University Press–Humphrey Milford1933https://archive.org/details/onehundredtenmir0000budg/mode/2up?view=theaterplates XXXVIII, XXXIX

Fol. 68r: Mary healing Mercurius (Märqorewos) from leprosy.Legends: አባ፡ ዘካርያስ፡ ሊቀ፡ ጳጳሳት፡ ዘገሠጾ፡ ለመርቆሬዎስ፡ ወከልኦ፡ እምቅዳሴ፡ በእንተ፡ ለምፁ። ዘከመ፡ አርአዮ፡ መርቆሬዎስ፡ ለምፀ፡ ሥጋሁ፡ ለሊቀ፡ ጳጳሳት። ዘከመ፡ ጸለየ፡ መርቆሬዎስ፡ ኀበ፡ ሥዕለ፡ እግዝእትነ፡ ማርያም። ዘከመ፡ አመረቶ፡ እግዝእትነ፡ ማርያም፡ ለመርቆሬዎስ፡ ከመ፡ ሐይወ፡ እምለምፁ። Cp. One Hundred and Ten Miracles of Our Lady Mary, Translated from Ethiopic Manuscripts for the Most Part in the British Museum, with Extracts from Some Ancient European Versions, and Illustrations from the Paintings in Manuscripts by Ethiopian Artists, with Sixty-Four PlatesErnest Alfred WallisBudgeLondonOxford University Press–Humphrey Milford1933https://archive.org/details/onehundredtenmir0000budg/mode/2up?view=theaterplates XL, XLI

Fol. 70r: Mary healing the broken foot of a woman.Legends: ዘከመ፡ ጾርዋ፡ ባዓራት፡ አዝማዲሃ፡ ለብእሲት፡ ስብርት፡ ወአብጽሕዋ፡ ውስተ፡ ቤተ፡ ክርስቲያን፡ ወገደፍዋ፡ ታሕተ፡ እግረ፡ ሥዕለ፡ እግዝእትነ። ዘከመ፡ ገደፍዋ፡ አዝማዲሃ፡ ታሕተ፡ እግረ፡ ሥዕለ፡ ለእግዝእትነ፡ ለብእሲት፡ ስብርት። ዘከመ፡ አንቅሀታ፡ እምንዋም፡ እግዝእትነ፡ ማርያም፡ በግሰታ፡ እግራ፡ ለብእሲት፡ ስብርት፡ ወአሕየወታ። ዘከመ፡ ተፈወሰት፡ ወቆመት፡ ቅድመ፡ ሥዕለ፡ ለእግዝእትነ። Cp. One Hundred and Ten Miracles of Our Lady Mary, Translated from Ethiopic Manuscripts for the Most Part in the British Museum, with Extracts from Some Ancient European Versions, and Illustrations from the Paintings in Manuscripts by Ethiopian Artists, with Sixty-Four PlatesErnest Alfred WallisBudgeLondonOxford University Press–Humphrey Milford1933https://archive.org/details/onehundredtenmir0000budg/mode/2up?view=theaterplates XLII, XLIII

Fol. 74r: Angels delivering Sophia (Sofya) of her child and giving it to Felix (Finǝks).Legends: ዘከመ፡ አውፅአ፡ መልአክ፡ ሕፃነ፡ እምከርሠ፡ ሶፍያ፡ እንዘ፡ ትነውም። ዘከመ፡ ወሀቦ፡ መልአክ፡ ለፊንክስ፡ ሕፃነ፡ ዘወፅአ፡ ከመ፡ ይሕፅኖ። Cp. One Hundred and Ten Miracles of Our Lady Mary, Translated from Ethiopic Manuscripts for the Most Part in the British Museum, with Extracts from Some Ancient European Versions, and Illustrations from the Paintings in Manuscripts by Ethiopian Artists, with Sixty-Four PlatesErnest Alfred WallisBudgeLondonOxford University Press–Humphrey Milford1933https://archive.org/details/onehundredtenmir0000budg/mode/2up?view=theaterplate XLIV

Fol. 74v: Widows accusing Sophia (Sofya) before Patriarch Severus (Sawiros).Legends: ሊቀ፡ ጳጳሳት፡ አባ፡ ሳዊሮስ። ዘከመ፡ አስተዋደይዋ፡ መበለታት፡ ለሶፍያ፡ ኀበ፡ ሊቀ፡ ጳጳሳት። ዘከመ፡ ነገረቶ፡ ሶፍያ፡ ለሊቀ፡ ጳጳሳት፡ በባሕቲቶሙ፡ ምሥጢረ፡ ፅንሰ፡ ወኵሎ፡ ዘኮነ። ዘከመ፡ ተመጠዎ፡ ሊቀ፡ ጳጳሳት፡ ለሕፃን፡ እምእደ፡ ፊንክስ። ሶበ: አዕረፈ፡ ሊቀ፡ ጳጳሳት፡ ዘገሠጻ፡ ለሶፍያ፡ ተሰይመ፡ ድኅሬሁ፡ በመንበሩ፡ ወልደ፡ ሶፍያ፡ ሊቀ፡ ጳጳሳት። Cp. One Hundred and Ten Miracles of Our Lady Mary, Translated from Ethiopic Manuscripts for the Most Part in the British Museum, with Extracts from Some Ancient European Versions, and Illustrations from the Paintings in Manuscripts by Ethiopian Artists, with Sixty-Four PlatesErnest Alfred WallisBudgeLondonOxford University Press–Humphrey Milford1933https://archive.org/details/onehundredtenmir0000budg/mode/2up?view=theaterplates XLV, XLVI

Fol. 76v: Mary and Barok.Legends: ዘከመ፡ ገብረ፡ በዓለ፡ ለባሮከ፡ እግዝእትነ፡ ማርያም፡ በውሂብ፡ ብዙኅ፡ ኅብስት፡ ወብዙኅ፡ ሜስ። ዘከመ፡ መጠዎ፡ ወይነ፡ ላእኩ፡ ለባሮክ። እለ፡ መስሑ፡ ሰብእ፡ በቤተ፡ ባሮክ። ቀሱታተ፡ ወይን፡ ዘሰትዩ፡ እምኔሆሙ፡ ሰብእ፡ በቤተ፡ ባሮክ። ዘከመ፡ ረገዞ፡ ፀሩ፡ ለባሮክ፡ በኵናት። ዝኒ፡ ዘወገሮ፡ በእብን፡ ለባሮክ። ዝኒ፡ ዘዘበጠ፡ በሰይፍ። ዘከመ፡ ገነዝዎ፡ እሉ፡ ሰብእ፡ ለባሮክ። Cp. One Hundred and Ten Miracles of Our Lady Mary, Translated from Ethiopic Manuscripts for the Most Part in the British Museum, with Extracts from Some Ancient European Versions, and Illustrations from the Paintings in Manuscripts by Ethiopian Artists, with Sixty-Four PlatesErnest Alfred WallisBudgeLondonOxford University Press–Humphrey Milford1933https://archive.org/details/onehundredtenmir0000budg/mode/2up?view=theaterplates XLVII, XLVIII, XLIX

Fol. 78v: Mary and Anastasius (ʾAnǝsṭasyos)Legends: ዘከመ፡ ጸለየ፡ አንስጣስዮስ፡ ቀዊሞ፡ ቅድመ፡ ሥዕለ፡ እግዝእትነ። ዘከመ፡ ተናገረቶ፡ እግዝእትነ፡ ማርያም፡ ነገረ፡ ፍሥሐ፡ ለአንስጣስዮስ፡ እንዘ፡ ሀሎ፡ ስኩበ፡ በደዌሁ። Cp. One Hundred and Ten Miracles of Our Lady Mary, Translated from Ethiopic Manuscripts for the Most Part in the British Museum, with Extracts from Some Ancient European Versions, and Illustrations from the Paintings in Manuscripts by Ethiopian Artists, with Sixty-Four PlatesErnest Alfred WallisBudgeLondonOxford University Press–Humphrey Milford1933https://archive.org/details/onehundredtenmir0000budg/mode/2up?view=theaterplate L

Fol. 82r: Monks beating ʾAbba Śalusi and forbidding him to eat beans.Legends: ዘከመ፡ ዘበጥዎ፡ ወረገፅዎ፡ መነኮሳት፡ ላአባ፡ ሣሉሲ። ዘከመ፡ ከልእዎ፡ መነኮሳት፡ በሊዓ፡ ተርሙስ፡ ለአባ፡ ሳሉሲ። ዘከመ፡ ጸለየ፡ አባ፡ ሣሉሲ። Cp. One Hundred and Ten Miracles of Our Lady Mary, Translated from Ethiopic Manuscripts for the Most Part in the British Museum, with Extracts from Some Ancient European Versions, and Illustrations from the Paintings in Manuscripts by Ethiopian Artists, with Sixty-Four PlatesErnest Alfred WallisBudgeLondonOxford University Press–Humphrey Milford1933https://archive.org/details/onehundredtenmir0000budg/mode/2up?view=theaterplates LI, LII, LIII

Fol. 86r: The cannibal of Qǝmǝr (bälaʾe säbʾ).Legends: ብእሲ፡ ባዕል፡ ዘሀገረ፡ ቅምር፡ ዘበልዓ፡ ፸ወ፰ ነፍሳት። ዘከመ፡ ጐዩ፡ ሰብእ፡ ዘሀገረ፡ ቅምር፡ ኃዲጎሙ፡ አብያቲሆሙ፡ ከመ፡ ኢይብልዖሙ፡ ... ዘከመ፡ ሐረዳ፡ በላዔ፡ ሰብእ፡ ለብእሲቱ፡ ወበልዓ፡ ሥጋሃ። ዘከመ፡ አስተዮ፡ ማየ፡ በላዔ፡ ሰብእ፡ ለነዳይ። Cp. One Hundred and Ten Miracles of Our Lady Mary, Translated from Ethiopic Manuscripts for the Most Part in the British Museum, with Extracts from Some Ancient European Versions, and Illustrations from the Paintings in Manuscripts by Ethiopian Artists, with Sixty-Four PlatesErnest Alfred WallisBudgeLondonOxford University Press–Humphrey Milford1933https://archive.org/details/onehundredtenmir0000budg/mode/2up?view=theaterplates LIV, LV, LVI

Fol. 86v: The cannibal’s soul rescued by Mary.Legends: ዘከመ፡ ነሥኡ፡ አጋንንት፡ ነፍሶ፡ ለበላዔ፡ ሰብአ። ዘከመ፡ አዘዘ፡ እግዝእትነ፡ ከመ፡ ይደደ፡ ነፍሰ፡ በላዔ፡ ሰብእ፡ ውስተ፡ ሲኦል። ዘከመ፡ ሰአለት፡ እግዝእትነ፡ ማርያም፡ ኀበ፡ ፍቁር፡ ወልዳ፡ ወመሐረ፡ ላቲ፡ ነፍስ፡ ውእቱ፡ በላዔ፡ ሰንእ፡ በምክንያተ፡ ሕፍነ፡ ማይ። ዘከመ፡ ደለወ፡ መልአከ፡ ነፍሳተ፡ ወሕፍነ፡ ማይ። ዘከመ፡ ነሥአታ፡ እግዝእትነ፡ ማርያም፡ ለነፍሰ፡ በላዔ፡ ሰብእ፡ እምእደ፡ አጋንንት፡ ወእታ፡ በእዴሃ። Cp. One Hundred and Ten Miracles of Our Lady Mary, Translated from Ethiopic Manuscripts for the Most Part in the British Museum, with Extracts from Some Ancient European Versions, and Illustrations from the Paintings in Manuscripts by Ethiopian Artists, with Sixty-Four PlatesErnest Alfred WallisBudgeLondonOxford University Press–Humphrey Milford1933https://archive.org/details/onehundredtenmir0000budg/mode/2up?view=theaterplate LVI

Fol. 88r: Mary rescuing a thief, a widow's son.Legends: ዘከመ፡ ቆመ፡ ዝኩ፡ ወልደ፡ መበለት፡ ሠራቂ፡ ቅድመ፡ ሥዕለ፡ እግዝእትነ፡ ማርያም፡ ድኅረ፡ ተፈትሐ፡ እምእሰሩ። ዘከመ፡ ሠረቀ፡ ወልደ፡ መበለት፡ ልብሰ፡ ቤተ፡ ክርስቲያን፡ ወሖረ፡ ጸዊሮ። ዘከመ፡ አኃዝዎ፡ ለዝኩ፡ ሠራቂ፡ ወልደ፡ መበለት፡ ሰብአ፡ ደብረ፡ ወአሰርዎ፡ እደዊሁ፡ ወእገሪሁ፡ ወፈቀዱ፡ ይምትሩ፡ በምጥባሕት፡ አባላቲሁ፡ ወአኅይገጽ[sic]። እግዝእትነ፡ ማርያም፡ ወፈትሕዎ፡ ወሖረ፡ ድኁነ። ዘከመ፡ አመረት፡ እግዝእትነ፡ ማርያም፡ በአዕባዕታ[sic]፡ ከመ፡ ይፍትሕዎ፡ ለሠራቂ። Cp. One Hundred and Ten Miracles of Our Lady Mary, Translated from Ethiopic Manuscripts for the Most Part in the British Museum, with Extracts from Some Ancient European Versions, and Illustrations from the Paintings in Manuscripts by Ethiopian Artists, with Sixty-Four PlatesErnest Alfred WallisBudgeLondonOxford University Press–Humphrey Milford1933https://archive.org/details/onehundredtenmir0000budg/mode/2up?view=theaterplate LVII

Fol. 90r: Mary protecting a woman from a flood who was bringing forth her child.Legends: ዘከመ፡ ጐዩ፡ ሰብእ፡ ፈሪሆሙ፡ ምልአተ፡ ማይ። ዘከመ፡ ይቤልዋ፡ እሉ፡ ሰብእ፡ ለይእቲ፡ ብእሲት፡ ፅንስት፡ እፎ፡ ኮንኪ፡ ማእከለ፡ ሞገደ፡ ባሕር። ዘከመ፡ ከደነታ፡ እግዝእትነ፡ ማርያም፡ በልብሰ፡ ብርሃን፡ ማእከለ፡ ሞገድ፡ ወረድአታ፡ ከመ፡ መወልዲት፡ እስከ፡ ወለደት፡ ወልደ፡ ማእከለ፡ ሞገድ።
Cp. One Hundred and Ten Miracles of Our Lady Mary, Translated from Ethiopic Manuscripts for the Most Part in the British Museum, with Extracts from Some Ancient European Versions, and Illustrations from the Paintings in Manuscripts by Ethiopian Artists, with Sixty-Four PlatesErnest Alfred WallisBudgeLondonOxford University Press–Humphrey Milford1933https://archive.org/details/onehundredtenmir0000budg/mode/2up?view=theaterplate LXI

Fol. 92v: Mary and a thirsty dog.Legends: ዘከመ፡ አስተየቶ፡ ማየ፡ እግዝእትነ፡ ማርያም፡ ለጽሙእ፡ ከልብ፡ ኀበ፡ ትትቀዳሕ፡ ማየ፡ ምስለ፡ ደናግል፡ ዘቤተ፡ መቅደስ። ደናግል፡ ዘቤተ፡ መቅደስ፡ ሐዋርያተ፡ ማይ፡ እላ፡ ሰደደሁ፡ ለውእቱ፡ ከልብ፡ ጽሙእ። ዘከመ፡ ዘበጠቶ፡ ወለት፡ ለጽሙእ፡ ከልብ፡ ወሰደደቶ፡ እማይ። Cp. One Hundred and Ten Miracles of Our Lady Mary, Translated from Ethiopic Manuscripts for the Most Part in the British Museum, with Extracts from Some Ancient European Versions, and Illustrations from the Paintings in Manuscripts by Ethiopian Artists, with Sixty-Four PlatesErnest Alfred WallisBudgeLondonOxford University Press–Humphrey Milford1933https://archive.org/details/onehundredtenmir0000budg/mode/2up?view=theaterplates LXII, LXIII, LXIV

Fols. 93v-94r: The Last Judgement (with quotations from Mt 25:34-46).Legends: ዘከመ፡ ይቤሎሙ፡ እግዚእነ፡ ለጻድቃን፡ ንዑ፡ ኀቤየ፡ ቡሩካኑ፡ ለአቡየ።
ይቤ፡ እግዚእነ፡ ለጻድቃን፡ እስመ፡ ርኅብኩ፡ አብላዕክሙኒ፡ ጻማእኩ፡ ወአስተይክሙኒ፡ ... ዘከመ፡ ይቤሎሙ፡ እግዝእነ፡ ለኃጥአን፡ ሑሩ፡ እምኔየ፡ ጉማን። ዝውእቱ፡ ዲያብሎስ፡ መፍቀሬ፡ ኃዝል። ሲኦል፡ ማኅደረ፡ አጋንንት።

item.desc: The title ተአምረ፡ ማርያም and the internal number of the monastic library (240)
Erasures are marked with parallel lines: e.g., fols. 32ra, 36vb, 95rab, 97ra.
Omitted words are written interlineally in a secondary hand: e.g., fols. 26ra, 28ra, 55vb, 80va, 84rab, 101vab.
Corrections are written over erased words and passages in a secondary hand: e.g., fols. 29ra,48vb, 53va, 85rb, 100va, 102vab (several lines).

binding.decoNote: Two wooden boards covered with reddish-brown tooled leather, textile inlays. Overback made of strong vermillion tooled leather. Two pairs of sewing stations.

The leather cover is decorated with metal (silver?) bosses. Blind tooled ornament on the leather cover (at least 8 tools were used: triple line fillet, double circle, corni-from, wave form, double crescent, double sine wave with dots, small x, cross hatching) Blind tooled ornament on the overback (at least 2 tools were used: double circle, wave form).

origin.origDate: King Fāsiladas (r. 1632-67) is mentioned as the reigning monarch on 68va, 69vb, 91va (Text I-1b). Texts I-1a and I-2 have been written more recently (19th cent.?), some leaves might have originally been left blank for miniatures.

history.provenance: Owner Ṣaggā Za-ʾAb is mentioned in the supplication formulas throughout Text I-1a (hand a). Walda Krǝstos, ʾƎlleni and ʾƎsṭifānos are mentioned throughout Text II (hand c).

projectDesc.p: Encoded according to TEI P5 Guidelines.

encodingDesc.p: Encoded according to the Beta maṣāḥǝft Guidelines. These Guidelines detail the TEI format ruled by the Beta maṣāḥǝft Schema. The present TEI file is enriched with an Xquery transformation taking advantage of the exist-db database instance where the data is stored and of the many external resources to which this data points to.

calendar.p: ʿĀmata ʿālam/ʿĀmata ʾəm-fəṭrat (Era of the World)
ʿĀmata śəggāwe (Era of the Incarnation – Ethiopian)

t:revisionDesc.change: transformed from mycore to TEI P5
adjusted to schema, no person ID provided
